title: Trump's Tarrifs in light of annexing countries -  Request

enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request to revisit his thoughts on Trump's annexation ambitions (Greenland, Canada, Panama Canal) now in light of Trump's tariffs. He argues both moves stem from the same pattern: an unqualified person in power who doesn't know how to govern, so they act chaotically. He expresses general support for the idea of tariffs to rebuild American manufacturing but thinks Trump is the wrong person to execute them. He then expands into a long digression on Greek/Roman political philosophy, class systems, meritocracy, and his idea of a history-test-based UBI system.
enrichment.speaker: kyle
topics: Elon Musk and tech billionaires in politics

stances.position: symptom of an unqualified leader who doesn't know how to govern, so he upends things just to seem consequential
personally supports the general idea of tariffs to bring manufacturing back to America and reduce global shipping
wrong person to do it — doing it all at once chaotically without understanding will cause economic damage
market/engineer class pretending to be aristocrats — their skill set is making money, not governing, and it leads to societal harm
explicitly supports 90% top tax rates and believes there should be no billionaires
favors a merit-based aristocracy rooted in education and statecraft, not inherited wealth or market success

stances.topic: UBI tied to a history test
stances.position: advocates giving UBI to people who pass a history test as a way to create a true meritocracy
compares Trump directly to Commodus and Caligula based on vanity and ignorance of history
